name: site_slang_translation_pack
purpose: Korean workplace slang and Japanese-derived field terms for ASR correction and context-aware translation.
notes: These terms should be used before general translation as glossary/context hints.
Many Japanese-derived field terms vary by region, trade, and company. Keep confidence and review flags visible in the admin UI.
If ASR hears variants, normalize to the canonical_ko field before translation.

terms.origin_hint: Likely from Japanese tesuri, meaning handrail/guardrail. Field confirmation required.
terms.plain_ko: 난간, 손잡이, 추락을 막는 가드레일을 뜻하는 현장 은어로 추정됩니다.

translations.vi: lan can hoặc thanh chắn an toàn
translations.th: ราวกันตกหรือราวจับนิรภัย
translations.km: របាំងសុវត្ថិភាព ឬ ដៃចាប់

terms.translation_rule: Do not transliterate. Translate by intended safety object: handrail/guardrail. If sentence concerns fall prevention, prefer guardrail.

terms.origin_hint: Japanese-derived construction term related to ink/chalk layout marking.
terms.plain_ko: 시공 위치를 표시하기 위해 바닥이나 벽에 먹줄/선을 치는 작업입니다.

translations.vi: đánh dấu đường mực/đường chuẩn thi công
translations.th: ตีเส้นกำหนดแนวงานก่อสร้าง
translations.km: គូសខ្សែសម្គាល់ខ្សែមូលដ្ឋានការងារ

terms.translation_rule: Translate as layout marking/chalk line marking, not as a person name or generic drawing.

terms.origin_hint: Japanese narashi, meaning leveling or smoothing.
terms.plain_ko: 바닥, 콘크리트, 흙, 자재 표면 등을 평평하게 고르는 작업입니다.

translations.vi: san phẳng hoặc làm phẳng bề mặt
translations.th: ปรับระดับหรือเกลี่ยพื้นผิวให้เรียบ
translations.km: កែសម្រួលកម្រិត ឬ ធ្វើឱ្យផ្ទៃរាបស្មើ

terms.translation_rule: Translate by task: leveling/smoothing. In concrete context, use concrete surface leveling; in soil context, use ground leveling.
